title: Pass pod restart policy to individual containers
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
When using a `RestartPolicy` together with a `livenessProbe`, the policy is now passed
down to the individual containers. Previously, this wasn't the case. (#20903)
```

---

Healthchecks, defined in a .yaml file as livenessProbe did not had any effect. They were executing as intended, containers were marked as unhealthy, yet no action was taken. This was never the intended behaviour, as observed by the following comment:

> if restart policy is in place, ensure the health check enforces it

A minimal example is tracked in #20903 with the following YAML:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ubi-httpd-24
spec:
  restartPolicy: Always
  containers:
    - name: ubi8-httpd
      image: registry.access.redhat.com/rhscl/httpd-24-rhel7:2.4-217
      livenessProbe:
        httpGet:
          path: "/"
          port: 8081
```

By passing down the restart policy (and using constants instead of actually wrong hard-coded ones), Podman actually restarts the container now.

comments.body:
> Your unit test is failing.

ah yeah, casted the wrong variable to int. my bad. should be fixed now. :3

> I am not sure this is the right approach. AFAIK the goal is the have the restart policy only on the pod level and not duplicated on each container as well.

First of all, thanks for the fast response! :blush: 

The thing is: If I understood the code flow right, the current implementation of the check never sees a different value than `none`, which is the explicit value passed from the init container. If my assumption is correct, the current, faulty code probably slipped past code review.

Maybe, there's a better way to solve the problem in #20903, that's up to you. I, as the user, can only report that it's solving the problem as intended. It's already running on my homeserver, so far without any problems. ^^

Changes LGTM.

BoltDB test is failing,  I suspect a flake and will restart.

> The thing is: If I understood the code flow right, the current implementation of the check never sees a different value than `none`, which is the explicit value passed from the init container. If my assumption is correct, the current, faulty code probably slipped past code review

Yes you are right the current code as is makes no sense, and your change is a valid fix. I just wasn't sure if the intend is to pass the policy down to each container or just have it use to upper pod policy instead. But reading the code it seems like we always pass it down when doeing podman pod create --restart as well so this here seems to match which is good.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hello Everyone,

Is there a specific reason that this commit was not included in the 5.0.2 release? 
I am facing the problem and I was wondering why it was not integrated in the release.

Thank you in advance and kind regards,
Vasileios

> Hello Everyone,
> 
> Is there a specific reason that this commit was not included in the 5.0.2 release? I am facing the problem and I was wondering why it was not integrated in the release.
> 
> Thank you in advance and kind regards, Vasileios

The simple answer nobody asked for it and we generally are selective about backports so nobody thought about including it. But we most likely create another release in the next few weeks we can include it there.

@Luap99: new pull request created: #22518

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/22003#issuecomment-2079480850):

>/cherry-pick v5.0
